2603.21430.title: DomAgent: Leveraging Knowledge Graphs and Case-Based Reasoning for Domain-Specific Code Generation
2603.21430.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) have shown impressive capabilities in code generation. However, because most LLMs are trained on public domain corpora, directly applying them to real-world software development often yields low success rates, as these scenarios frequently require domain-specific knowledge. In particular, domain-specific tasks usually demand highly specialized solutions, which are often underrepresented or entirely absent in the training data of generic LLMs. To address this challenge, we propose DomAgent, an autonomous coding agent that bridges this gap by enabling LLMs to generate domain-adapted code through structured reasoning and targeted retrieval. A core component of DomAgent is DomRetriever, a novel retrieval module that emulates how humans learn domain-specific knowledge, by combining conceptual understanding with experiential examples. It dynamically integrates top-down knowledge-graph reasoning with bottom-up case-based reasoning, enabling iterative retrieval and synthesis of structured knowledge and representative cases to ensure contextual relevance and broad task coverage. DomRetriever can operate as part of DomAgent or independently with any LLM for flexible domain adaptation. We evaluate DomAgent on an open benchmark dataset in the data science domain (DS-1000) and further apply it to real-world truck software development tasks. Experimental results show that DomAgent significantly enhances domain-specific code generation, enabling small open-source models to close much of the performance gap with large proprietary LLMs in complex, real-world applications. The code is available at: https://github.com/Wangshuaiia/DomAgent.
2603.21430.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在代碼生成方面展現了令人印象深刻的能力。<lb/>然而，由於大多數LLMs是在公共領域的語料庫上訓練的，直接將它們應用於現實世界的軟體開發通常會產生低成功率，因為這些情境經常需要特定領域的知識。<lb/>特別是，特定領域的任務通常需要高度專業化的解決方案，而這些解決方案在通用LLMs的訓練數據中往往被低估或完全缺失。<lb/>為了解決這一挑戰，我們提出了DomAgent，一個自主編碼代理，通過結構化推理和針對性檢索使LLMs能夠生成適應特定領域的代碼，從而彌補這一差距。<lb/>DomAgent的一個核心組件是DomRetriever，一個新穎的檢索模塊，它模擬人類如何學習特定領域的知識，通過結合概念理解與經驗範例。<lb/>它動態地將自上而下的知識圖譜推理與自下而上的案例推理整合在一起，使得結構化知識和代表性案例的迭代檢索和綜合成為可能，以確保上下文的相關性和廣泛的任務覆蓋。<lb/>DomRetriever可以作為DomAgent的一部分運作，也可以獨立於任何LLM進行靈活的領域適應。<lb/>我們在數據科學領域的開放基準數據集（DS-1000）上評估了DomAgent，並進一步將其應用於現實世界的卡車軟體開發任務。<lb/>實驗結果顯示，DomAgent顯著增強了特定領域的代碼生成，使得小型開源模型能夠在複雜的現實世界應用中縮小與大型專有LLMs之間的性能差距。<lb/>代碼可在以下網址獲得：https://github.com/Wangshuaiia/DomAgent。
2603.21430.author: Shuai Wang et.al.
2603.21430.authors: Shuai Wang, Dhasarathy Parthasarathy, Robert Feldt, Yinan Yu
